fwupd:hsi-installed.name: fwupd installed
fwupd:hsi-installed.description:
Checks that fwupd is installed on the host.

Hosts without fwupd report a firmware update status of FWUPD_MISSING and cannot provide firmware security inventory. This is a prerequisite for all other firmware security checks.

rules.attribute: Firmware update status

fwupd:hsi-level-1.name: HSI Level 1+ - Critical firmware protections
fwupd:hsi-level-1.description: Rolled-up check: the host achieves at least HSI Level 1 (Critical). See the individual Level 1 conditions for specific checks.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI level

fwupd:hsi-level-2.name: HSI Level 2+ - Important firmware protections
fwupd:hsi-level-2.description: Rolled-up check: the host achieves at least HSI Level 2 (Important). See the individual Level 2 conditions for specific checks.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI level

fwupd:hsi-level-3.name: HSI Level 3+ - Recommended firmware protections
fwupd:hsi-level-3.description: Rolled-up check: the host achieves at least HSI Level 3 (Recommended). See the individual Level 3 conditions for specific checks.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI level

fwupd:hsi-level-4.name: HSI Level 4 - Complete firmware protections
fwupd:hsi-level-4.description: Rolled-up check: the host achieves HSI Level 4 (Complete). See the individual Level 4 conditions for specific checks.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI level

fwupd:no-pending-updates.name: No pending firmware updates
fwupd:no-pending-updates.description:
Checks that the host has no outstanding firmware updates.

Hosts with pending firmware updates have known firmware versions that should be applied. Use the manage-fwupd module to automate firmware update application.

rules.attribute: Firmware update status

fwupd:status-ok.name: Firmware status healthy
fwupd:status-ok.description:
Checks that fwupd is present, has discoverable devices, and no pending updates.

This is the strictest firmware status check. A host passes only when fwupd is installed, reports at least one updatable device, and has no outstanding firmware updates.

rules.attribute: Firmware update status

fwupd:hsi-l1-uefi-secure-boot.name: UEFI SecureBoot
fwupd:hsi-l1-uefi-secure-boot.description:
Secure Boot is enabled and functional.

UEFI Secure Boot prevents unauthorized code from running during the boot process by verifying digital signatures of boot loaders and drivers.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: UEFI secure boot

fwupd:hsi-l1-tpm-v2.0.name: TPM 2.0 Present
fwupd:hsi-l1-tpm-v2.0.description:
TPM 2.0 device exists and is enabled.

A Trusted Platform Module provides hardware-backed cryptographic operations and secure measurement storage for verified boot.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: TPM v2.0

fwupd:hsi-l1-tpm-empty-pcrs.name: Empty PCR in TPM
fwupd:hsi-l1-tpm-empty-pcrs.description:
All TPM PCRs 0-7 have valid measurements.

Empty Platform Configuration Registers indicate that the firmware boot process is not being measured, weakening the chain of trust.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: TPM empty PCRs

fwupd:hsi-l1-uefi-platform-key.name: UEFI Platform Key
fwupd:hsi-l1-uefi-platform-key.description:
Valid production platform key is installed (not a test key).

The UEFI Platform Key (PK) is the root of trust for Secure Boot. A test key allows any code to be signed and run.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: UEFI platform key

fwupd:hsi-l1-bios-firmware-updates.name: BIOS Capsule Updates
fwupd:hsi-l1-bios-firmware-updates.description:
Firmware update mechanism (UEFI capsule updates) is enabled.

When disabled, the system cannot receive firmware security patches through the standard UEFI update mechanism.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: BIOS firmware updates

fwupd:hsi-l1-spi-write.name: BIOS Write Enable (BWE)
fwupd:hsi-l1-spi-write.description:
SPI ROM is protected from userspace writes.

The BIOS Write Enable bit controls whether the SPI flash containing the BIOS can be written from the operating system. It should be disabled to prevent firmware tampering.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: SPI write

fwupd:hsi-l1-spi-lock.name: BIOS Lock Enable (BLE)
fwupd:hsi-l1-spi-lock.description:
SMI protection on BIOSWE bit changes is enabled.

BIOS Lock Enable generates a System Management Interrupt when software attempts to change the BIOS Write Enable bit, preventing silent firmware tampering.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: SPI lock

fwupd:hsi-l1-spi-bios-region.name: SMM BIOS Write Protect
fwupd:hsi-l1-spi-bios-region.description:
BIOS region is non-writable outside System Management Mode.

SMM BIOS Write Protect ensures that the BIOS SPI flash region cannot be modified except from within the trusted SMM environment.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: SPI BIOS region

fwupd:hsi-l1-spi-descriptor.name: Read-only SPI Descriptor
fwupd:hsi-l1-spi-descriptor.description:
SPI flash descriptor region is locked.

The SPI descriptor defines access permissions for SPI flash regions. If unlocked, an attacker could modify these permissions to gain write access to the BIOS.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: SPI descriptor

fwupd:hsi-l1-supported-cpu.name: Supported CPU
fwupd:hsi-l1-supported-cpu.description:
Platform CPU has HSI test support in fwupd.

Unsupported CPUs cannot have their firmware security attributes fully evaluated.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: Supported CPU

fwupd:hsi-l1-platform-debugging.name: Platform Debug (Intel DCI)
fwupd:hsi-l1-platform-debugging.description:
USB3 debugging interface (Intel DCI) is disabled.

Direct Connect Interface allows deep hardware debugging over USB3. If enabled in production, it provides an attack surface for physical access exploits.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: Platform debugging

fwupd:hsi-l1-csme-manufacturing-mode.name: ME Manufacturing Mode
fwupd:hsi-l1-csme-manufacturing-mode.description:
Intel Management Engine is not in manufacturing mode.

Manufacturing mode is intended for factory provisioning. If left enabled, it exposes additional attack surface on the ME subsystem.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: csme manufacturing mode

fwupd:hsi-l1-csme-override.name: ME Flash Descriptor Override
fwupd:hsi-l1-csme-override.description:
Intel ME flash descriptor override (debug mode) is not accessible.

The override strap allows bypassing SPI flash access controls, enabling firmware tampering through physical access.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: csme override

fwupd:hsi-l1-mei-key-manifest.name: ME BootGuard Platform Key
fwupd:hsi-l1-mei-key-manifest.description:
CPU fuses are not using leaked or compromised keys.

The MEI key manifest validates that the BootGuard keys burned into the CPU fuses have not been compromised.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: MEI key manifest

fwupd:hsi-l1-csme-version.name: CSME Version
fwupd:hsi-l1-csme-version.description:
Intel Converged Security and Management Engine firmware is not vulnerable to known critical CVEs.

Outdated CSME versions may contain exploitable vulnerabilities that compromise the hardware root of trust.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: CSME version

fwupd:hsi-l1-uefi-bootservice-vars.name: UEFI BootService Variables
fwupd:hsi-l1-uefi-bootservice-vars.description:
Boot-only UEFI variables are not readable at OS runtime.

BootService variables should be inaccessible after ExitBootServices() to prevent information leakage to the operating system.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: UEFI bootservice variables

fwupd:hsi-l1-amd-microcode-signature.name: AMD Microcode Signature
fwupd:hsi-l1-amd-microcode-signature.description:
Firmware prevents loading of maliciously crafted CPU microcode.

AMD processors vulnerable to microcode signature verification bypass (EntrySign) allow arbitrary microcode patches, undermining hardware security guarantees.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: AMD microcode signature

fwupd:hsi-l1-platform-fused.name: Part is Fused
fwupd:hsi-l1-platform-fused.description:
Hardware one-time-programmable fuses are blown for tampering protection.

Production hardware should have security fuses blown to lock down debugging interfaces and enforce boot chain verification.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: Part is fused

fwupd:hsi-l1-smm-locked-down.name: SMM Locked Down
fwupd:hsi-l1-smm-locked-down.description:
System Management Mode save state protection prevents data exfiltration.

SMM lockdown prevents SMM handlers from reading or modifying OS memory, closing a class of privilege escalation attacks.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L1: SMM locked down

fwupd:hsi-l2-iommu.name: DMA Protection (IOMMU)
fwupd:hsi-l2-iommu.description:
IOMMU is enabled for PCIe DMA attack prevention.

The IOMMU restricts DMA-capable devices to their assigned memory regions, preventing malicious peripherals from reading or writing arbitrary system memory.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: IOMMU

fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-bootguard.name: Intel BootGuard Enabled
fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-bootguard.description:
Intel BootGuard feature is activated.

BootGuard provides hardware-rooted verified boot by validating the Initial Boot Block before execution.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: Intel BootGuard

fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-bootguard-verified.name: Intel BootGuard Verified
fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-bootguard-verified.description:
Intel BootGuard boot chain verification is functional.

Verified Boot mode ensures each stage of the boot process is cryptographically validated before execution.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: Intel BootGuard verified boot

fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-bootguard-acm.name: Intel BootGuard ACM
fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-bootguard-acm.description:
Intel Authenticated Code Modules are protected.

ACMs are signed microcode modules executed by the CPU before any other code. Their integrity is essential for the hardware root of trust.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: Intel BootGuard ACM protected

fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-bootguard-otp.name: Intel BootGuard OTP
fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-bootguard-otp.description:
SOC is locked via one-time programmable fuses for BootGuard.

OTP fuses permanently bind the BootGuard key hash to the CPU, preventing key substitution attacks.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: Intel BootGuard OTP fuse

fwupd:hsi-l2-tpm-pcr0-reconstruction.name: PCR0 TPM Event Log
fwupd:hsi-l2-tpm-pcr0-reconstruction.description:
TPM PCR0 value matches the reconstructed firmware measurements.

PCR0 reconstruction verifies that the TPM event log is consistent with the actual PCR values, detecting tampering with boot measurements.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: TPM PCR0 reconstruction

fwupd:hsi-l2-bios-rollback-protection.name: BIOS Rollback Protection
fwupd:hsi-l2-bios-rollback-protection.description:
BIOS prevents installing older vulnerable firmware versions.

Without rollback protection, an attacker with firmware write access could downgrade to a version with known vulnerabilities.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: BIOS rollback protection

fwupd:hsi-l2-platform-debugging.name: Part is Debug Locked
fwupd:hsi-l2-platform-debugging.description:
Hardware debugging access is locked.

Debug interfaces (JTAG, etc.) allow full hardware access. In production systems these should be permanently locked to prevent physical attack vectors.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: Platform debugging

fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-gds-mitigation.name: Intel GDS Mitigation
fwupd:hsi-l2-intel-gds-mitigation.description:
CPU microcode patches mitigate Gather Data Sampling.

GDS (Downfall) is a transient execution side-channel vulnerability in Intel CPUs that can leak data across security boundaries.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: Intel GDS mitigation

fwupd:hsi-l2-amd-platform-secure-boot.name: AMD Platform Secure Boot
fwupd:hsi-l2-amd-platform-secure-boot.description:
AMD Platform Secure Boot prevents non-manufacturer firmware execution.

The AMD Secure Processor validates firmware authenticity before allowing execution, providing a hardware root of trust.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: AMD platform secure boot

fwupd:hsi-l2-amd-spi-write-protections.name: AMD SPI Write Protections
fwupd:hsi-l2-amd-spi-write-protections.description:
SPI bus write control prevents unauthorized firmware changes on AMD systems.

AMD SPI write protections lock the SPI flash to prevent unauthorized firmware modifications.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: AMD SPI write protections

fwupd:hsi-l2-hp-surestart.name: HP SureStart
fwupd:hsi-l2-hp-surestart.description:
HP SureStart BIOS self-healing and corruption detection is enabled.

HP SureStart monitors the BIOS for corruption or tampering and automatically restores it from a protected copy.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L2: HP SureStart

fwupd:hsi-l3-suspend-to-idle.name: Suspend-to-Idle
fwupd:hsi-l3-suspend-to-idle.description:
Default sleep state is suspend-to-idle (S0ix) rather than S3.

Suspend-to-idle keeps the IOMMU and other security features active during sleep, unlike S3 which powers down protections.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L3: Suspend-to-idle

fwupd:hsi-l3-suspend-to-ram.name: Suspend to RAM Disabled
fwupd:hsi-l3-suspend-to-ram.description:
S3 suspend-to-RAM sleep is not available or not the default.

S3 sleep powers down the IOMMU, disabling DMA protections and creating a window for cold boot or DMA attacks on resume.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L3: Suspend-to-ram

fwupd:hsi-l3-pre-boot-dma-protection.name: Pre-boot DMA Protection
fwupd:hsi-l3-pre-boot-dma-protection.description:
IOMMU is configured for pre-boot DMA attack mitigation.

Pre-boot DMA protection ensures the IOMMU is active before the OS loads, preventing DMA attacks during early boot when the OS IOMMU driver is not yet running.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L3: Pre-boot DMA protection

fwupd:hsi-l3-intel-bootguard-policy.name: Intel BootGuard Policy
fwupd:hsi-l3-intel-bootguard-policy.description:
BootGuard error enforcement policy halts boot on verification failure.

In enforcement mode, BootGuard stops the boot process if signature verification fails rather than continuing with potentially compromised firmware.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L3: Intel BootGuard error policy

fwupd:hsi-l3-cet-platform.name: CET Available
fwupd:hsi-l3-cet-platform.description:
Control Flow Enforcement Technology is supported and enabled.

CET provides hardware-enforced shadow stacks and indirect branch tracking to mitigate return-oriented programming (ROP) and jump-oriented programming (JOP) attacks.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L3: CET Platform

fwupd:hsi-l3-amd-spi-replay-protections.name: AMD SPI Replay Protections
fwupd:hsi-l3-amd-spi-replay-protections.description:
Monotonic counter prevents SPI flash replay attacks on AMD systems.

SPI replay protection uses a hardware counter to detect when an older SPI flash image has been re-applied, preventing firmware downgrade attacks.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L3: AMD SPI replay protections

fwupd:hsi-l3-uefi-memory-protections.name: Early-boot UEFI Memory Protections
fwupd:hsi-l3-uefi-memory-protections.description:
NX and read-only protections are applied to UEFI boot memory.

Early-boot memory protections prevent code injection and data corruption during the UEFI boot phase by enforcing W^X (write XOR execute) policies.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L3: UEFI memory protections

fwupd:hsi-l4-encrypted-ram.name: DRAM Memory Encryption
fwupd:hsi-l4-encrypted-ram.description:
Total Memory Encryption (Intel TME) or Secure Memory Encryption (AMD SME) encrypts all data on the memory bus.

Memory encryption protects against physical memory access attacks including cold boot attacks and DMA-based memory scraping.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L4: Encrypted RAM

fwupd:hsi-l4-smap.name: SMAP
fwupd:hsi-l4-smap.description:
Supervisor Mode Access Prevention is enabled.

SMAP prevents the kernel from accidentally accessing user-space memory, mitigating a class of privilege escalation exploits that trick the kernel into dereferencing user-controlled pointers.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L4: SMAP

fwupd:hsi-l3-cet-os-support.name: CET Utilized by OS
fwupd:hsi-l3-cet-os-support.description:
The operating system actively uses Control Flow Enforcement Technology.

While CET hardware support (Level 3) is necessary, the OS must also enable and use it for the protection to be effective.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L3: CET OS Support

fwupd:hsi-l4-amd-rollback-protection.name: AMD Secure Processor Rollback
fwupd:hsi-l4-amd-rollback-protection.description:
AMD Secure Processor prevents firmware downgrade attacks.

The AMD Platform Security Processor maintains anti-rollback counters that prevent installation of older firmware versions with known vulnerabilities.

rules.attribute: Firmware HSI L4: AMD rollback protection
